speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Well said, my noble Scot. If speaking truth

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In this fine age were not thought flattery,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Such attribution should the Douglas have

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As not a soldier of this season’s stamp

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Should go so general current through the world.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By God, I cannot flatter. I do defy

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The tongues of soothers. But a braver place

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In my heart’s love hath no man than yourself.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nay, task me to my word; approve me, lord.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Thou art the king of honor.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: No man so potent breathes upon the ground

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: But I will beard him.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Do so, and ’tis well.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text:
What letters hast thou there?
                 
                To
                 
                Douglas
                .
               I can but thank you.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: These letters come from your father.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Letters from him! Why comes he not himself?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He cannot come, my lord. He is grievous sick.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Zounds, how has he the leisure to be sick

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In such a justling time? Who leads his power?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Under whose government come they along?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: His letters bears his mind, not I, my lord.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I prithee, tell me, doth he keep his bed?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He did, my lord, four days ere I set forth,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And, at the time of my departure thence,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He was much feared by his physicians.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: I would the state of time had first been whole

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Ere he by sickness had been visited.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: His health was never better worth than now.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Sick now? Droop now? This sickness doth infect

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The very lifeblood of our enterprise.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: ’Tis catching hither, even to our camp.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He writes me here that inward sickness—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And that his friends by deputation

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Could not so soon be drawn, nor did he think it meet

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To lay so dangerous and dear a trust

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: On any soul removed but on his own;

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Yet doth he give us bold advertisement

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That with our small conjunction we should on

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To see how fortune is disposed to us,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: For, as he writes, there is no quailing now,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Because the King is certainly possessed

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of all our purposes. What say you to it?

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Your father’s sickness is a maim to us.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A perilous gash, a very limb lopped off!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And yet, in faith, it is not. His present want

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Seems more than we shall find it. Were it good

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To set the exact wealth of all our states

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: All at one cast? To set so rich a main

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: On the nice hazard of one doubtful hour?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: It were not good, for therein should we read

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The very bottom and the soul of hope,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The very list, the very utmost bound

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of all our fortunes.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Faith, and so we should, where now remains

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: A sweet reversion. We may boldly spend

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Upon the hope of what is to come in.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: A comfort of retirement lives in this.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A rendezvous, a home to fly unto,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If that the devil and mischance look big

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Upon the maidenhead of our affairs.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: But yet I would your father had been here.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The quality and hair of our attempt

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Brooks no division. It will be thought

speaker: WORCESTER
text: By some that know not why he is away

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That wisdom, loyalty, and mere dislike

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Of our proceedings kept the Earl from hence.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And think how such an apprehension

speaker: WORCESTER
text: May turn the tide of fearful faction

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And breed a kind of question in our cause.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: For well you know, we of the off’ring side

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Must keep aloof from strict arbitrament,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And stop all sight-holes, every loop from whence

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The eye of reason may pry in upon us.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: This absence of your father’s draws a curtain

speaker: WORCESTER
text: That shows the ignorant a kind of fear

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Before not dreamt of.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: You strain too far.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I rather of his absence make this use:

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: It lends a luster and more great opinion,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A larger dare, to our great enterprise

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Than if the Earl were here, for men must think

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If we without his help can make a head

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To push against a kingdom, with his help

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: We shall o’erturn it topsy-turvy down.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Yet all goes well; yet all our joints are whole.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: As heart can think. There is not such a word

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Spoke of in Scotland as this term of fear.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My cousin Vernon, welcome, by my soul.

speaker: VERNON
text: Pray God my news be worth a welcome, lord.

speaker: VERNON
text: The Earl of Westmoreland, seven thousand strong,

speaker: VERNON
text: Is marching hitherwards, with him Prince John.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: No harm, what more?

speaker: VERNON
text: And further I have learned

speaker: VERNON
text: The King himself in person is set forth,

speaker: VERNON
text: Or hitherwards intended speedily,

speaker: VERNON
text: With strong and mighty preparation.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He shall be welcome too. Where is his son,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The nimble-footed madcap Prince of Wales,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And his comrades, that daffed the world aside

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And bid it pass?

speaker: VERNON
text: All furnished, all in arms,

speaker: VERNON
text: All plumed like estridges that with the wind

speaker: VERNON
text: Bated like eagles having lately bathed,

speaker: VERNON
text: Glittering in golden coats like images,

speaker: VERNON
text: As full of spirit as the month of May,

speaker: VERNON
text: And gorgeous as the sun at midsummer,

speaker: VERNON
text: Wanton as youthful goats, wild as young bulls.

speaker: VERNON
text: I saw young Harry with his beaver on,

speaker: VERNON
text: His cuisses on his thighs, gallantly armed,

speaker: VERNON
text: Rise from the ground like feathered Mercury

speaker: VERNON
text: And vaulted with such ease into his seat

speaker: VERNON
text: As if an angel dropped down from the clouds,

speaker: VERNON
text: To turn and wind a fiery Pegasus

speaker: VERNON
text: And witch the world with noble horsemanship.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: No more, no more! Worse than the sun in March

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: This praise doth nourish agues. Let them come.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: They come like sacrifices in their trim,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And to the fire-eyed maid of smoky war

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: All hot and bleeding will we offer them.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The mailèd Mars shall on his altar sit

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Up to the ears in blood. I am on fire

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To hear this rich reprisal is so nigh

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And yet not ours. Come, let me taste my horse,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Who is to bear me like a thunderbolt

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Against the bosom of the Prince of Wales.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Harry to Harry shall, hot horse to horse,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Meet and ne’er part till one drop down a corse.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O, that Glendower were come!

speaker: VERNON
text: There is more news.

speaker: VERNON
text: I learned in Worcester, as I rode along,

speaker: VERNON
text: He cannot draw his power this fourteen days.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: That’s the worst tidings that I hear of yet.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Ay, by my faith, that bears a frosty sound.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: What may the King’s whole battle reach unto?

speaker: VERNON
text: To thirty thousand.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Forty let it be.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: My father and Glendower being both away,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: The powers of us may serve so great a day.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come, let us take a muster speedily.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Doomsday is near. Die all, die merrily.

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Talk not of dying. I am out of fear

speaker: DOUGLAS
text: Of death or death’s hand for this one half year.
